jokes.text: Oh, God. I hate impeachments. They're so '90s.
jokes.setup: Serious political discussion about impeachment proceedings
jokes.punchline: Selina treats impeachment like a fashion trend that's passé
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Selina's narcissistic superficiality reduces serious constitutional crisis to aesthetic preference. Sharp, economical writing.

jokes.text: Oh, these lips are on lockdown. Right. You don't know. No, I just have a 'don't ask don't tell' policy about the things that I know. Or that I don't know.
jokes.setup: Jonah pretends to have important inside information
jokes.punchline: His convoluted non-explanation reveals he knows nothing
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah - pompous incompetence masquerading as importance. The escalating nonsense works but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: You need to fuck off and go back to Westworld.
jokes.setup: Selina dismissing Jonah
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference suggests Jonah is an android
jokes.explanation: Relies on pop culture reference for humor rather than character-specific wit. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: Three fucks, you're out.
jokes.setup: Selina has said 'fuck off' three times
jokes.punchline: Baseball reference twisted to profanity count
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay with perfect economy. Works as both escalation and callback to the repeated dismissals.

jokes.text: Ma'am, you have my deepest, deepest sympathies. Okay, well, I'm not dead.
jokes.setup: Gary offers condolences for Selina leaving politics
jokes.punchline: Selina's literal correction of his funeral-level sympathy
jokes.explanation: Gary's characteristic over-emotional response meets Selina's cutting literalism. Good character interplay.

jokes.text: Can you please tell Governor Chung that Dan Egan is ready for the Chung chat?
jokes.setup: Dan making a professional phone call
jokes.punchline: Awkward alliteration reveals his desperation for the job
jokes.explanation: Dan's smarmy self-importance on full display. The alliteration feels forced but that's the point - he's trying too hard.

jokes.text: Still, this might mean that we could spend more time together. Let's take it one day at a time.
jokes.setup: Ed suggesting they could spend more time together
jokes.punchline: Amy's non-committal response that clearly means 'no'
jokes.explanation: Subtle relationship humor showing Amy's complete disinterest. Works but not memorable.

jokes.text: The father figure my own dad was too weird and distant to be.
jokes.setup: Mike explaining his relationship with his potential new boss
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of daddy issues in job interview
jokes.explanation: Mike's tendency to overshare inappropriately. The casual delivery of heavy emotional content is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You know, you're gonna get bedsores.
jokes.setup: Mike saying he could read newspapers in bed 'for the rest of his life'
jokes.punchline: Practical medical concern about permanent bed rest
jokes.explanation: Unexpected literal interpretation of Mike's romantic metaphor. Sharp turn from emotional to medical.

jokes.text: They call me the Washington Flash. I doubt that.
jokes.setup: Will boasting about his speed
jokes.punchline: Amy's immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Simple setup-punchline with Amy's characteristic bluntness. Effective but unremarkable.

jokes.text: I move as slowly as a Mississippi detective investigating the murder of a young black man.
jokes.setup: Asked to describe how slowly he moves
jokes.punchline: Dark social commentary about police indifference
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific. Takes what could be throwaway physical comedy and elevates it to sharp social satire. Unexpected and memorable.

jokes.text: Wish I could break his spine. But I can't, 'cause it's made of Jell-O.
jokes.setup: Gary saying Selina will break the president's heart
jokes.punchline: Selina's violent wish thwarted by the president's supposed spinelessness
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina - violent impulses met with contemptuous assessment of target's weakness. The Jell-O metaphor is vivid and memorable.

jokes.text: Are you finishing my sentence or are you telling me that again? Both.
jokes.setup: Ben says 'fuck off' after Selina's sentence
jokes.punchline: Ben's matter-of-fact confirmation it's both finishing her thought and an insult
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy gold. Ben's casual acknowledgment that he's simultaneously being helpful and insulting is perfectly deadpan.

jokes.text: You're like a Viking stuck in time.
jokes.setup: Mike trying to sell himself as valuable
jokes.punchline: Chung's assessment of Mike as anachronistic warrior
jokes.explanation: Vivid metaphor for obsolescence. The Viking imagery is specific and memorable, though the joke setup is straightforward.

jokes.text: No, I'm being serious, too. I'd like to fuck her.
jokes.setup: Mike asking seriously about Charlize Theron connections
jokes.punchline: Chung's crude honesty about his motivations
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that works for character but relies on shock value rather than wit. The misdirection is effective but cheap.

jokes.text: Why'd you leave anyway? You get stuck with your dick in the veep's decaf?
jokes.setup: Asking why Mike left his job
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor mixing sexual scandal with coffee service
jokes.explanation: Chung's crude directness. The coffee/sex metaphor is creative but relies heavily on shock value.

jokes.text: Why don't you fucking kill yourself, Will?
jokes.setup: Will interrupting with information
jokes.punchline: Chung's casual suggestion of suicide
jokes.explanation: Shock value comedy that reveals Chung's casual cruelty. Effective for character but lazy writing technique.

jokes.text: No, I'm Joan fucking Rivers. Of course I'm serious.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if he's serious about major news
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic reference to deceased comedian
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic retort using pop culture reference. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: While in your mind you're doing pirouettes on his grave.
jokes.setup: Ben noting Selina's fake sadness about the president
jokes.punchline: Vivid imagery of her mental celebration
jokes.explanation: Beautifully specific metaphor that perfectly captures Selina's barely contained glee. Ben's cynical insight into her psychology.

jokes.text: Well, it's not the job that's depressing. Life is depressing.
jokes.setup: Ben saying she's getting out of a depressing job
jokes.punchline: Selina's broader philosophical pessimism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - taking any opportunity to express existential dread. The economy and delivery make it work.

jokes.text: (suppressed squeal) Yes! Fuck! Gosh!
jokes.setup: Selina trying to maintain composure after good news
jokes.punchline: Her unsuccessful attempt to suppress celebration, escalating from squeal to profanity to innocent exclamation
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy moment showing Selina's struggle between public composure and private joy. The progression of exclamations is well-crafted.

jokes.text: You do and Secret Service might break your neck. That would be a tragedy.
jokes.setup: Furlong threatening to enter through side door
jokes.punchline: Sue's dry suggestion that his injury would be tragic
jokes.explanation: Sue's characteristic dry wit. The deadpan delivery of obviously insincere sympathy is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm burning calories with the old anxiety diet. Oh. You know, the anxiet.
jokes.setup: Furlong asking if she's working out
jokes.punchline: Selina's made-up portmanteau of 'anxiety' and 'diet'
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that captures Selina's tendency to coin terms. The self-awareness about her anxiety is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: If you want to get some cardio exercise, you have to have a heart.
jokes.setup: Discussion about exercise
jokes.punchline: Implying Selina is heartless
jokes.explanation: Basic wordplay insult. Functional but predictable setup-punchline structure.

jokes.text: Oh, I heard it from the Gingerhead Man. The gash with the stash. McLintock.
jokes.setup: Identifying his source of information
jokes.punchline: Creative insults playing on McLintock's appearance
jokes.explanation: Creative nickname construction. 'Gingerhead Man' and 'gash with the stash' show inventive wordplay, though it's purely insulting.

jokes.text: Mike sees knowledge as the enemy.
jokes.setup: Dismissing Mike as source of information
jokes.punchline: Perfectly captures Mike's relationship with competence
jokes.explanation: Perfect character assassination that's both funny and accurate. Great economy.

jokes.text: It's like saying flammable or inflammable. It's the same thing. I smell a fucking rat.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to explain her word choice confusion
jokes.punchline: Furlong immediately seeing through her linguistic dodge
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Selina's attempt to cover her slip, immediately punctured by Furlong's suspicion. The flammable comparison is clever misdirection.

jokes.text: Then we'll find out whether it's flammable or inflammable. Come on, Wilbo Faggins, let's go.
jokes.setup: Callback to Selina's flammable/inflammable comparison
jokes.punchline: Furlong uses her own metaphor as threat, plus Tolkien-based insult for Will
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that weaponizes Selina's own metaphor. 'Wilbo Faggins' is juvenile but fits Furlong's character.

jokes.text: You like to have sex and you like to travel? Yes, ma'am. Then you can fuck off.
jokes.setup: Seeming to offer Jonah something good based on his interests
jokes.punchline: Combines his interests into dismissal
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection setup-punchline. The structure is perfect - builds expectation then delivers the dismissal. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Like the mighty oak that stands for-- ma'am.
jokes.setup: Jonah beginning grandiose metaphor about his loyalty
jokes.punchline: Cut off mid-sentence by Selina's entrance
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah pomposity deflated by timing. The interruption comedy works because it prevents his usual long-winded nonsense.

jokes.text: Selina Meyer, she deals 'em fire. Bam! Boom! Let's go to the fucking moon!
jokes.setup: Team celebrating Selina's presidential run
jokes.punchline: Dan's over-the-top enthusiasm with nonsensical space reference
jokes.explanation: Dan's sycophantic enthusiasm escalating to absurdity. The moon reference is random but captures his manic excitement.

jokes.text: I was this close to living on my boat.
jokes.setup: Team celebrating staying together
jokes.punchline: Mike's specific vision of his backup plan
jokes.explanation: Classic Mike - his backup plan is oddly specific and slightly pathetic. Sets up his boat obsession for later.

jokes.text: Can you give me some time with my core team? Um, Br-- Br- Ed. Bre-- Ed. Of course.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to dismiss Ed politely
jokes.punchline: Her inability to remember his name despite multiple attempts
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy. Selina's multiple failed attempts to remember Ed's name while he stands right there is painfully authentic.

jokes.text: They're celebrating because you weren't in there.
jokes.setup: Ed asking what happened during the celebration
jokes.punchline: Amy's brutal honesty about his exclusion
jokes.explanation: Amy's signature brutal honesty. The economy is perfect - she answers his question with devastating directness.

jokes.text: They're celebrating because you weren't in there.
jokes.setup: Ed asking what happened in the meeting
jokes.punchline: Amy's brutal honesty about his exclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy cruelty with excellent economy. Cuts right to the point.

jokes.text: Did you say a number? Currently four.
jokes.setup: Dan mentioning he accepted posts
jokes.punchline: Reveals he accepted multiple jobs simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan - his opportunism is so extreme he's accepted four jobs. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. You're choosing dead milk over me?
jokes.setup: Gary explaining his cheese business
jokes.punchline: Selina's disgusted description of cheese
jokes.explanation: 'Dead milk' is a brilliantly disgusting but accurate description of cheese. Perfectly captures Selina's dramatic outrage at Gary's betrayal.

jokes.text: Like I'm Marilyn Monroe, just JFK the fuck out of me.
jokes.setup: Wanting to make her speech more presidential
jokes.punchline: Sexual reference to JFK/Monroe affair as speech coaching
jokes.explanation: Selina's inappropriate sexualization of presidential imagery. Using 'JFK' as a verb is creative and memorable.

jokes.text: Hey, quit being so hot. You're gonna melt all my cheese.
jokes.setup: Dana greeting Gary at the cheese display
jokes.punchline: Combining flirtation with literal cheese business
jokes.explanation: Functional sexual innuendo that works with the cheese setting. Not particularly clever but establishes Dana's aggressive personality.

jokes.text: You look like you did when I asked you to talk dirty. You were just like, 'I'm in you right now.'
jokes.setup: Dana noting Gary's uncomfortable expression
jokes.punchline: Callback to Gary's awkward dirty talk attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary characterization - even his dirty talk is awkward and literal. Dana's casual revelation is cringe-inducing in the best way.

jokes.text: You're on the good ship Pecorino now.
jokes.setup: Gary saying that ship has sailed on working for Selina
jokes.punchline: Dana's nautical metaphor using cheese name
jokes.explanation: Clever combination of 'good ship' metaphor with specific cheese name. Works better than expected for the context.

jokes.text: It's like I'm her central nervous system.
jokes.setup: Gary explaining his importance to Selina
jokes.punchline: Medical metaphor for his essential role
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary - his self-importance manifested through biological metaphor. The central nervous system comparison is both accurate and pathetic.

jokes.text: Honey, she'll get another bag bunny, I just know it.
jokes.setup: Gary claiming to be essential
jokes.punchline: Dana's dismissive term for Gary's role
jokes.explanation: 'Bag bunny' is a perfect dismissive term for Gary's bag-carrying duties. Creative and cutting.

jokes.text: What the fuck is wrong with you, Brett? This is not a big place, is it? It's not a big place.
jokes.setup: Brett the customer confused about cheese locations
jokes.punchline: Dana's mounting frustration with simple directions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Dana's control issues. The repetition of 'not a big place' emphasizes her exasperation.

jokes.text: You, Chief of Staff to the president, me, chief of sexiness and spooning.
jokes.setup: Amy talking about career focus
jokes.punchline: Andrew's made-up title combining politics and intimacy
jokes.explanation: Andrew's attempt to insert himself into Amy's political ambitions through sexual role. Character-appropriate sleaziness.

jokes.text: Yeah, it's like a noun-verb gumbo.
jokes.setup: Amy criticizing Dan's speech writing
jokes.punchline: Dan's food metaphor for his bad grammar
jokes.explanation: 'Noun-verb gumbo' is a perfect metaphor for terrible writing - random ingredients thrown together. Shows Dan's self-awareness.

jokes.text: Why don't you change America to Shmamerica?
jokes.setup: Dan's minimal changes to avoid plagiarism
jokes.punchline: Amy's sarcastic suggestion of equally pointless change
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Amy's frustration. The 'Shm-' construction effectively mocks Dan's lazy solution.

jokes.text: It's an ant kibbutz.
jokes.setup: Student showing science project
jokes.punchline: Combining ant farm with Jewish collective living concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant combination of ant farm and kibbutz concepts. Shows genuine cleverness from the student character.

jokes.text: Well, from up here, they look like ants. That's a joke. Because they are ants.
jokes.setup: Selina making observation about ant project
jokes.punchline: Her painful over-explanation of obvious joke
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - making an obvious observation then explaining why it's supposed to be funny. The meta-commentary makes it worse.

jokes.text: You're at a science fair. One of these kids could build you a charger.
jokes.setup: Mike needing phone charger
jokes.punchline: Logical solution using available resources
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor about the setting. Amy's practical solution highlights the obvious resources available.

jokes.text: So now our spineless, flip-floppy fuckbag is staying.
jokes.setup: Explaining political consequences
jokes.punchline: Ben's triple-insult description of the president
jokes.explanation: Ben's signature vicious efficiency. Three different insults building to 'fuckbag' - perfect escalation and economy.

jokes.text: I've got to get out of here before I set fire to one of these nerds.
jokes.setup: Selina frustrated at science fair
jokes.punchline: Violent threat against innocent children
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina - inappropriate violent imagery directed at children. The casual delivery makes it funnier and more disturbing.

jokes.text: (Spanish accent) 'Mr. Ching, I come to America to work. I send money home, but life here is so hard for me.'
jokes.setup: Chung explaining his made-up anecdote
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical immigrant impression with wrong name
jokes.explanation: Problematic racial humor that relies on stereotypes. Shows Chung's casual racism but the comedy itself is weak and offensive.

jokes.text: Jesus, the inside of his head must be like an Escher drawing.
jokes.setup: POTUS changing his mind repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Visual art reference for mental confusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor combining art history with political psychology. The Escher reference perfectly captures impossible, contradictory mental structure. Highly quotable and intelligent.

jokes.text: See this right here? You know what this is? This is me playing 'Fuck You' on the world's biggest cello.
jokes.setup: Chung dismissing Dan condescendingly
jokes.punchline: Dan's elaborate metaphor for his dismissive gesture
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant metaphor construction. Takes a simple dismissive gesture and elevates it to orchestral grandeur. Peak Dan pomposity channeled into genuine wit.

jokes.text: If I had a dollar for every time you mentioned that goddamn war, I would buy a tank and I would blow your fat fucking head off.
jokes.setup: Frustrated with Chung's constant war references
jokes.punchline: Violent fantasy using war metaphor against veteran
jokes.explanation: Dan's rage building to elaborate violent fantasy. The irony of using war imagery against the veteran is well-crafted, though dark.

jokes.text: That's Washington, DC, for you. DC-- District of Cunts.
jokes.setup: Discussing how everyone gets screwed in Washington
jokes.punchline: Alternative meaning for DC abbreviation
jokes.explanation: Clever acronym redefinition that perfectly captures Dan's cynical worldview. Simple but effective and memorable.

jokes.text: We're like Area 51 except more restricted.
jokes.setup: Jonah describing restricted office area
jokes.punchline: Comparing mundane office to famous secret facility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah - inflating the importance of basic office security. The comparison is absurd but fits his grandiose self-image.

jokes.text: Information-wise, I am becalmed.
jokes.setup: Asked about presidential visit
jokes.punchline: Nautical metaphor misapplied to lack of information
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jonah-ism. 'Becalmed' is perfectly pretentious while being oddly appropriate. His tendency to use fancy words incorrectly.

jokes.text: You've got to mello yellow. Don't tell me not to panic.
jokes.setup: Gary trying to calm Selina down
jokes.punchline: Gary's attempt to say 'mellow out' becomes soda reference
jokes.explanation: Gary's nervous malapropism turning calming phrase into Mountain Dew reference. Perfect character moment showing his stress.

jokes.text: Everybody can just shut the shit up.
jokes.setup: Selina in panic mode
jokes.punchline: Mixing 'shut the fuck up' and 'shut up' into nonsense
jokes.explanation: Selina's stress causing linguistic breakdown. The mixed profanity shows her mental state perfectly.

jokes.text: Has he been miniaturized?
jokes.setup: Told POTUS is 'right in that room' but can't see him
jokes.punchline: Scientific explanation for president's absence
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Selina logic. Takes the sci-fi context and applies it literally to solve her confusion.

jokes.text: Is it Bring a Disparate Person to Work Day?
jokes.setup: Seeing unusual mix of people in office
jokes.punchline: Creating fake holiday to explain the chaos
jokes.explanation: Creative made-up holiday that perfectly describes the situation. 'Disparate' is a great word choice - formal but accurate.

jokes.text: Selina is about to get a Potal bullet through the head and you are all about to be unemployed and homeless.
jokes.setup: Jonah explaining the political situation
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'POTUS' while making assassination metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah - trying to sound important while mispronouncing basic terms. 'Potal bullet' is perfectly wrong.

jokes.text: You're the secretary to the vice president. That's like being Garfunkel's roadie, okay?
jokes.setup: Ed insulting Sue's position
jokes.punchline: Comparing her job to roadie for lesser Simon & Garfunkel member
jokes.explanation: Brilliant layered insult - roadie to the less famous member of the duo. Perfect specificity and cultural knowledge creating maximum dismissiveness.

jokes.text: I mean, it was just, you know, RIP-OTUS. Just complete sinking shit.
jokes.setup: Describing the president's political death
jokes.punchline: Combining 'RIP' with 'POTUS' acronym
jokes.explanation: 'RIP-OTUS' is brilliant wordplay combining death terminology with presidential acronym. Highly quotable and clever construction.

jokes.text: And so it begins. Yeah, with a kick to the tits.
jokes.setup: Amy noting the campaign begins with plagiarism scandal
jokes.punchline: Ben's crude metaphor for the bad start
jokes.explanation: Ben's characteristic crude directness. The phrase is memorable and captures the immediate setback perfectly.

jokes.text: I think that the president should only have her staff here. Or his staff. You know, their staff.
jokes.setup: Selina describing ideal West Wing
jokes.punchline: Her revealing slip about gender expectations, then over-correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Freudian slip revealing Selina's presidential ambitions, followed by awkward over-correction. Shows her psychology beautifully.

jokes.text: Was your mom plagiarizing the Bible when she said, 'Oh, God, oh, God'?
jokes.setup: Defending against plagiarism charges
jokes.punchline: Sexual interpretation of religious exclamation
jokes.explanation: Mike's crude defense strategy mixing religion and sex. Shows his desperation and poor judgment but gets a laugh.

jokes.text: Oh, no, you don't. You want to be president.
jokes.setup: Child says she wants to be VP like Selina
jokes.punchline: Selina corrects her ambitions upward
jokes.explanation: Good ending moment showing Selina's ambition and mentoring. Sweet but funny.

jokes.text: Oh, no, you don't. You want to be president.
jokes.setup: Child saying they want to be VP like her
jokes.punchline: Selina correcting their ambition upward
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending moment showing Selina's growth and ambition. Sweet moment that also reinforces her presidential goals.
